MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Nicholas Burke, Esq., of BURNHAM BROWN (510-444-6800), appearing for defendant / moving party Borg-Warner Corporation by its successor-in-interest Borg Warner Morse Tec, Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Ruth Cash, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT BORG-WARNER CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No argument made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Borg-Warner Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied and it's alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the first cause of action (labeled indemnity) and denied as to the second cause of action (labeled contribution). As to the first cause of action, defendant has satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs have not presented any evidence creating a triable fact that GM and Ford are entitled to complete indemnity from defendant since the undiputed fact is that any liability that defendant may have for Charles Cash's death is not the result of GM or Ford OEM parts attributable to defendant. As to the second cause of action, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the portion of the GM and Ford settlement payments attributable to economic damages was more than GM and Ford's proportional share of the total economic damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
